comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: FORCE_CLAIM: plan-review gate bypassed by user approval — no plan-review attestation was verified. Reason: "plan-review PASSED (signed, non-blocking) at origin/main 81a15f4d minutes ago; attestation staled only by this stack's own unrelated c366 commit advancing HEAD to 37ab727 (mechanical HEAD-freshness, not a plan change/BLOCK). Gate intent satisfied; claiming all before further commits.".

compiled_state.description:
## Why

`compact.emit_legacy_signature_mirror` (default False) is the CONTRACT-phase rollback
lever of the additive-attestations rollout (epic dark-acme-lumen). Its default already
means "never emit the legacy `state['signature']` mirror into new snapshots" — so
removing it does NOT change runtime behavior. What removal deletes is the documented
one-line ROLLBACK (`= true` + recompact) that re-emits the mirror for a still-live
pre-attestations reader. Approved for removal; this story does it responsibly.

## What

- Remove the field + validator from `CompactConfig` (`_config_schema.py:367` and the
  parser entry in the `compact` section, `~:515`).
- Hardcode never-emit in `_snapshot_strip_keys` (`_commands/compact.py:127-136`):
  always add `"signature"` to the dropped-keys set; delete the `emit_legacy_mirror`
  branch.
- PRESERVE the in-memory mirror path: `process_signature` re-deriving `state["signature"]`
  during replay (`reducer/_processors.py:562-604`) and the `most_recent_attestation`
  fallback (`signing.py:563-568`) stay — this story only stops PERSISTING the mirror
  into new snapshots; it does not touch the in-memory back-compat projection.
- Doc-sweep coordination: the doc-sweep story removes references; this story updates
  `docs/migrations.md` "Legacy signature-mirror retirement" to record the retirement
  and that the rollback lever is gone.

## Scope — the one-way door (handle, don't skip)

Backward-compat verdict (from assessment): CONDITIONAL. Removal is a ONE-WAY DOOR. It is
safe for THIS fleet (hourly auto-update to origin/main ⇒ no pre-attestations binary is
live). It WOULD break an external project on older rebar that reads a mirror-less
snapshot whose latest signature was compacted below the horizon (the reader sees no
`signature`, cannot read `attestations`, and the SIGNATURE event is gone) — after
removal the only recovery is a code downgrade, not a config flip.

Therefore, before landing: assert the readiness gate (`docs/migrations.md:149-156`)
holds — no pre-attestations clone/reconcile-host shares this store — and record the
one-way-door retirement in the release notes so any downstream consumer is warned.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `emit_legacy_signature_mirror` removed from schema, parser, and `rebar.toml` (if present); new snapshots never persist the `signature` mirror.
- [ ] `process_signature` in-memory mirror + `most_recent_attestation` fallback are UNCHANGED (prove with existing signing/replay tests green).
- [ ] [operator-attested] Readiness gate asserted: no pre-attestations rebar binary reads this store (state the basis — e.g. fleet auto-update policy).
- [ ] `docs/migrations.md` "Legacy signature-mirror retirement" updated to record the retirement and removal of the rollback lever; release notes warn downstream consumers of the one-way door.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.
compiled_state.title: Retire compact.emit_legacy_signature_mirror (hardcode never-emit) after readiness-gate check
